description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 72-77.

Legendary Warlock

Every deal has two sides. They are not always born out of desperation; a moment of weakness in the night, where someone gives something precious for something binding. Sometimes, a deal merely represents two parties recognizing, with perfect clarity, that they need each other. A patron reaches across the vast and howling dark not because a mortal begged, but because their attention was piqued. And, in the long history of powers beyond mortal comprehension making bets on mortal lives, some of these deals become much more profitable than others.

After an entire life of adventuring, who exactly got the better end of that deal?

A Legendary Warlock has spent their career in a relationship that most people would find difficult to explain. A constant negotiation with a being that doesn’t always have their best interests in mind. The warlock has delivered the expected results consistently, across twenty levels of impossible situations, and the power flowing through them reflects that track record. They are no longer considered a vassal, but a partner, and sometimes even an equal. Whether the Patron has fully admitted it yet or not, the balance of that partnership struck at the beginning of the Warlock’s adventure has shifted in ways that render the logic of servitude moot.

The few Legendary Warlocks that walk the cosmos have come to alter their deal. They carry a power intimate, built over struggle. Those who began as slaves have come to the top and inverted their bonds. Those who spent their lives pleasing their patron are now part of that creature’s inner circle. Each relationship is different, each unique.

At 20th level, the warlock has become the ultimate representation of their patron’s power. A chosen crusader, an unstoppable reaper, an endorsed overlord; the power a Legendary Warlock commands has long since outgrown the original terms of the agreement. They have survived things their Patron did not anticipate. Learned things their Patron did not intend to teach. Arrived at a level of capability that places them, whether either party planned for it, somewhere in the vicinity of the beings they once struck a deal with.

Score Improvement (3), Supernatural Intervention

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Warlock table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary Invocations

Also at 20th level, you have discovered Legendary Eldritch Invocations, great and terrible secrets from your Patron that increase your power substantially. You gain one Legendary Invocation of your choice.

You gain an additional Legendary Eldritch Invocation when you reach 22nd, 24th, and 25th level, as shown in the Warlock Table.

Cantrips

Invocations

Legendary Invocations Slots

Prepared

Spell Slot

Known

Known

Level

Spells

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Persistent Arcanum

Starting at 21st level, you gain a deeper understanding of the functioning of your pact magic. You gain an additional 5th level spell slot (included on the class table).

When you complete a short rest, you regain any expended uses of your 6th and 7th-level Mystic Arcanums. In addition, you learn one additional Mystic Arcanum option for each Mystic Arcanum up to 9th level. When you cast a Mystic Arcanum, you can choose either option.

Level 22

Legendary Arcanum

Beginning at 22nd level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret of peerless power, a mark of the trust and responsibility you have accrued in its service. Choose one 10th-level spell from the warlock spell list as your legendary arcanum. You can cast your legendary arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you must also expend 2 Legendary Points. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

Patron’s Influence

Also at 22nd level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point and use your reaction when a creature that you can see makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw to either add or subtract your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) from the result.

Level 23

Patron’s Predilection

At 23rd level, you are bestowed a special power to escape deadly danger. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to take no damage instead and immediately cast the Plane Shift spell, targeting only yourself, without expending a spell slot. You appear in a pocket dimension that belongs to your Patron, where they can communicate with you directly. While in this mystical space, you cannot be reduced to 0 hit points and you end all conditions and spell effects of your choice currently afflicting you. You can remain in this pocket dimension for a maximum of 1 hour. You can use an action to cast plane shift on yourself again without expending a spell slot to return to the exact location you left, or choose another plane of existence.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Eldritch Invocation Savant

You gain 2 additional Eldritch Invocations. When you complete a long rest, you can change 1 Eldritch Invocation for another eligible option.

This feature does not affect Legendary Eldritch Invocations.

Level 24

Arcanism of the Outer Planes

Starting when you reach 24th level, your Patron gifts you with a powerful arcanism that you can manifest into the world, a spell that best represents its energy and power. Depending on your Patron, you can cast one of the following 9th-level spells by expending 2 Legendary Points. Once you cast this spell, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest. If you are playing a subclass other than the ones listed below, work with your DM to choose a 9th-level spell that best represents your Patron. Alternatively, choose one of the following.

Patron’s Arcanism

Patron 9th-level spell

The Fiend Meteor Swarm

The Archfey Gate

The Great Old One Foresight

Level 25

Supernatural Intervention

Beginning at 25th level, the scale of your service to your patron has earned you the right to invoke its personal help, if only momentarily. You can call upon your patron’s supernatural intervention by using your action and expending 2 Legendary Points. An Avatar of your patron (as per the Avatar statblock on page 125) appears in an unoccupied space within 120 feet of you. Your patron can speak through it, and it has a telepathy range of 120 feet. If you are in combat, it acts on your initiative count. While within 500 feet of your patron’s avatar, you and your allies are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions.

Your patron’s avatar remains for 24 hours or until it is destroyed. Once you have beseeched your patron’s supernatural intervention, you must wait 7 days before you can call upon it again.

Legendary Eldritch Invocations

Blade of Perdition

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock, Thirsting Blade Invocation

The Extra Attack granted by your Thirsting Blade invocation confers three extra attacks rather than one. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend a spell slot to deal additional damage equal to twice your level as Dark damage.

Death Guard

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock

You learn the Death Ward spell. You can cast it on yourself without expending a spell slot.

Legendary Blast

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock, a Warlock Cantrip That Deals Damage

Choose a warlock cantrip that deals damage with a casting time of 1 action. When you cast that cantrip, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to perform a Legendary Blast instead. When you do so, you make only one spell attack with a range of 120 feet. On a hit, you deal 4d10 x 5 force damage. On a critical hit, you do not multiply the damage by 2, but instead deal 6d10 x 5 force damage.

When you hit with this special attack, you inflict any additional effects normally applied by your cantrip. If you possess Eldritch Invocations that modify your cantrip’s range, they also modify your Legendary Blast. Legendary Blast counts as a higher-tier effect for the purposes of Tiers of Magic.

In addition, when you cast your chosen cantrip, you ignore any Limited Magic Immunity that would render a creature immune to cantrip-level spells.

Repeatable. You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.

Patron’s Awareness

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock

You gain truesight out to 30 feet. If you already have truesight, its range increases by 30 feet. In addition, you gain blindsight equal to your truesight range.

When you roll Initiative, your result cannot be lower than 30, as your patron’s foresight compels you to act.

Quickened Blast

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock, a Warlock Cantrip That Deals Damage

Choose a warlock cantrip that deals damage with a casting time of 1 action. That cantrip now has a casting time of 1 bonus action instead. If you use the chosen cantrip to perform a Legendary Blast, you can perform that Legendary Blast as a bonus action.

Repeatable. You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.

Shield of Dead Gods

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock

Your AC becomes 30. You cannot further increase your AC by any means, except through temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less.

Warlock’s Servant

Prerequisite: Level 20+ Warlock

Choose a creature of CR 10 or lower whose type is related to your Patron, as determined by your DM. That creature becomes your servant. As an action, you can summon it in an unoccupied space within 60 feet of you. It is an ally to you and your companions, rolls its own Initiative, and acts on its own turn. The servant remains for 1 hour or until it is destroyed or dismissed. When reduced to 0 hit points or dismissed, it disappears, leaving behind anything it was wearing or carrying. Your servant’s presence counts as a higher-tier magical effect for the purposes of Tiers of Magic.

Once you summon your servant, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest. You can change your chosen servant whenever you gain a Legendary Warlock level, provided the new creature meets the prerequisites.

Patron’s Technique

Prerequisite: Level 22+ Warlock

Your patron confides in you a Mythical Technique of your choice from the Paladin or Rogue list.

Repeatable. You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible Mythical Technique.

Soul Blast

Prerequisite: Level 22+ Warlock, a Warlock Cantrip That Deals Damage

Choose a warlock cantrip that deals damage. That cantrip gains one additional damage die and deals Soul damage instead of its normal damage type.

If you use the chosen cantrip to perform a Legendary Blast, your Legendary Blast deals 6d10 x 5 Soul damage. On a critical hit, you do not multiply the damage by 2, but instead deal 8d10 x 5 Soul damage.

Repeatable. You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.

Strike of the Covenant

Prerequisite: Level 22+ Warlock, Thirsting Blade Invocation

The strength of your patron manifests in a single eldritch strike. You can use your action and expend 1 Legendary Point to perform a Strike of the Covenant. Make a single melee weapon attack against a creature within range, with the reach of your weapon increased to 30 feet. You have a +10 bonus to hit on this attack.

On a hit, the attack deals 10d8 x 5 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) instead of your weapon’s normal damage. This attack does not benefit from any other bonus to its damage rolls. On a critical hit, you do not multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal 13d8 x 5 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice). You regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt.

Once you perform a Strike of the Covenant, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Warlock’s Champion

Prerequisite: Level 22+ Warlock, Warlock’s Servant Invocation

Your servant can remain for up to 8 hours, and its CR can be up to 16. You can retain a lower CR servant if you choose, and work with your DM to bring it up to a fitting CR 16 stat block.

Warp Gate

Prerequisite: Level 22+ Warlock

You can cast Teleport at will, targeting only yourself and one additional willing creature, without expending a spell slot.

Prison of Realms Beyond

Prerequisite: level 24+ Warlock

You can expend 2 Legendary Points to cast the Imprisonment spell innately, without expending a spell slot. If you do so, Imprisonment has a casting time of 1 action for you. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Warlock’s Wish

Prerequisite: level 24+ Warlock

You can expend 2 Legendary Points to cast the Wish spell at 9th level innately, without expending a spell slot. If you do so, you do not suffer the repercussions of casting the Wish spell. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again for 30 days.

Patron’s Relic

Prerequisite: Level 25+ Warlock

Your patron creates a Relic-level magic item and bestows it upon you. Work with your DM to determine its nature.

Warlock’s Chosen

Prerequisite: Level 25+ Warlock, Warlock’s Champion Invocation

Your servant remains at your side indefinitely until destroyed or dismissed. If destroyed, you cannot summon it again until you finish a long rest. Your servant’s CR can be up to 21. You can retain a lower CR servant if you choose, and work with your DM to bring it up to a fitting CR 21 stat block.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
